deploy/charts/benthos-collector/template.purpose: Helm templates that render all Kubernetes resources for the benthos-collector StatefulSet; owns the full resource set (StatefulSet, Service, RBAC, ServiceAccount, Secret, ConfigMap) and enforces the naming, config-injection, and leader-election wiring conventions for the collector binary.

patterns.name: All names go through _helpers.tpl macros
patterns.description: Every resource name uses `include "benthos-collector.fullname"` or `include "benthos-collector.componentName"` — never raw `.Release.Name` or `.Chart.Name` concatenation.

patterns.name: All labels use the common label helpers
patterns.description: Every `metadata.labels` block calls `include "benthos-collector.labels" . | nindent 4`; selector labels use `include "benthos-collector.selectorLabels" . | nindent N`.

patterns.name: Config is stored in a Secret, not a ConfigMap
patterns.description: The Benthos config.yaml is base64-encoded into a Secret (name: `<fullname>-config`) and mounted at `/etc/benthos/config.yaml` as a read-only subPath volume; never stored in a ConfigMap.

patterns.name: benthos-collector.args helper resolves startup mode
patterns.description: Container args are always emitted via `include "benthos-collector.args" .`; the helper validates that exactly one of `config`, `configFile`, or `preset` is set and fails the render otherwise.

patterns.name: Downward-API K8s metadata injected as env vars
patterns.description: The StatefulSet always injects K8S_POD_NAME, K8S_POD_UID, K8S_NAMESPACE, K8S_APP_INSTANCE, K8S_APP_NAME, K8S_APP_VERSION via fieldRef so the benthos binary can self-identify for leader election.
patterns.example:
- name: K8S_POD_NAME
  valueFrom:
    fieldRef:
      fieldPath: metadata.name

patterns.name: Credentials live in a mandatory Secret rendered unconditionally
patterns.description: secret.yaml always renders two Secrets: one for OPENMETER_URL/OPENMETER_TOKEN (required .Values.openmeter.url fails if absent) and one for the Benthos config; both are referenced by the StatefulSet via `envFrom.secretRef` and volume.
patterns.example: OPENMETER_URL: {{ required "OpenMeter URL is required" .Values.openmeter.url | b64enc | quote }}

patterns.name: Config change triggers pod roll via checksum annotation
patterns.description: The StatefulSet pod template carries `checksum/config` and `checksum/secret` annotations computed from `.Values.config | toYaml | sha256sum` and `.Values.openmeter | toYaml | sha256sum` so pods roll on config changes.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all named template functions: name, fullname, chart, labels, selectorLabels, serviceAccountName, componentName (two-arg), storageClass, args. Single source of truth for naming logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: `benthos-collector.componentName` takes a list `(list . "component")` not a dict — calling it with wrong arg type causes a cryptic nil-pointer in render.

key_file_guides.role: Core workload; uses StatefulSet (not Deployment) to support persistent storage and stable pod names for leader election; mounts config Secret as subPath, injects Downward API env, and conditionally adds PVC volumeClaimTemplates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Storage is controlled by `.Values.storage.enabled`; if omitted, the `data` volumeMount and volumeClaimTemplates are skipped entirely — adding a volume without gating on this flag will break renders when storage is disabled.

key_file_guides.role: Always renders two Secrets unconditionally; the config Secret holds the full Benthos YAML. Changing config Secret name breaks the volume reference in statefulset.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The config Secret name is computed via `benthos-collector.componentName (list . "config")` — if this macro changes, the volumeMount in statefulset.yaml must be updated in sync.

key_file_guides.role: Conditionally (`.Values.rbac.create`) creates ClusterRole+Binding (for pod/node/PVC reads) plus a namespace-scoped Role+Binding for lease management (leader election).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Contains a duplicate `subjects:` key in the ClusterRoleBinding block — this is an existing YAML bug that some parsers accept; do not replicate the pattern in new resources.

key_file_guides.role: Only renders when `.Values.caRootCertificates` is non-empty; stores CA certs with names derived from the map key + `.crt` suffix, lowercased.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If CA cert names in the ConfigMap change, the corresponding volumeMount path in statefulset.yaml (`/usr/local/share/ca-certificates`) must still match.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding release or chart name strings instead of using `benthos-collector.fullname` / `benthos-collector.componentName`
Storing the Benthos config.yaml in a ConfigMap — it is always a Secret to protect potential credentials embedded in the config
Adding container args directly to statefulset.yaml instead of routing them through the `benthos-collector.args` helper, which validates config source exclusivity
Adding new env vars without checking whether they conflict with the mandatory Downward-API set (K8S_POD_NAME, K8S_NAMESPACE, etc.) injected unconditionally
Using a Deployment instead of StatefulSet — the workload requires stable pod identity for leader election and optionally persistent storage via volumeClaimTemplates

decisions.rationale: Stable pod names (K8S_POD_NAME from Downward API) are required for the leader-election lease mechanism; PVC volumeClaimTemplates also require StatefulSet.

decisions.decision: Config stored in Secret, not ConfigMap
decisions.rationale: The Benthos config can contain sensitive credentials (API keys, tokens); Secret keeps them base64-encoded and subject to RBAC, unlike a ConfigMap.
StatefulSet does not automatically roll pods on Secret/ConfigMap changes; sha256sum annotations force a pod roll when config or openmeter credentials change.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new named template helper and using it in a resource
